title: How to calculate elapsed years as a decimal (YEARFRAC)
task: Get the fraction of a year between two dates — accruals, prorating, service length.

excel.note: Basis 1 = actual/actual (real calendar). Basis 0 (default) is the 30/360 bond convention.

explanation: YEARFRAC turns two dates into decimal years. The basis argument picks the day-count convention: 0 (default) is 30/360 — every month counts as 30 days, so Jan 1 to Jul 1 is exactly 0.5 — while basis 1 uses the actual calendar (181/365 ≈ 0.4959 for the same dates). Use 30/360 for bond-style finance, actual/actual for real elapsed time like tenure or prorated fees. A simpler approximation, (B2-A2)/365.25, is fine when the convention doesn't matter.
